frameworks.name: decoded neurofeedback (DecNef)
frameworks.one_line: closed-loop fMRI paired with machine-learning decoder to reinforce target neural patterns

frameworks.name: closed-loop fMRI
frameworks.one_line: real-time fMRI feedback paradigm enabling participant-driven neural modulation

core_question: Can a public multi-study DecNef dataset enable broader investigation of factors underlying non-invasive modulation of brain dynamics?
key_assumptions: decoder-trained sessions provide reliable target patterns for subsequent neural reinforcement
across-study aggregation preserves enough commonality for re-analysis
manipulations: data combined across 5 separate published DecNef studies
per participant: one decoder-training session plus 3-10 closed-loop reinforcement sessions
key_findings: release of fMRI data from 5 prior DecNef studies covering >60 participants
each participant contributes a decoder-training session and multiple closed-loop neural reinforcement sessions
interpretations: the curated public dataset can support the fMRI community in studying mechanisms of non-invasive brain-dynamics modulation
